analyst_observations: New skill achieves 100% pass rate vs 16.7% for old skill — a massive improvement.
The discriminating assertions are: task decomposition (A1/B1/C1), sequential Agent calls (A2/B2/C2), progress tracking (A4/B4), user checkpoints (B3), and clean shutdown (C4).
Old skill only passes cycle summary (A3) and session summary (C3) — basic reporting works in both versions.
Orchestration overhead is the key difference: old skill uses ~12 API calls per cycle (TeamCreate+TaskCreate+TaskUpdate+SendMessage), new skill uses 3 Agent calls per cycle.
Token usage is nearly identical (+4.5%), confirming the new skill's simplification doesn't add cost.
Duration is virtually the same (-0.3%), so the simplification has no performance penalty.
